# Figure description: 4_Карта_Прогнозов_M-42-IX.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-IX/4_Карта_Прогнозов_M-42-IX.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **forecast/prognosis geological map** ("Карта прогнозов") with elements showing the regularities of mineral deposit placement ("с элементами закономерностей размещения полезных ископаемых"). It is a thematic predictive geological map compiled at 1:200,000 scale, overlaying geological mapping with mineral prospectivity zonation.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers sheet **M-42-IX** of the Soviet topographic/geological grid system, located in northern Kazakhstan. It was produced in **1984** by the **Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение** (Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association) under the **Министерство геологии Казахской ССР** (Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR). The map is designated **Чертёж №36** (Drawing No. 36) and bears archive stamp **36572**, consistent with the report context. The compiler is identified as **Науменко В.В.** (Naumenko V.V.), compiled at the Central Geological-Prospecting Expedition ("Центральная геолого-поисковая экспедиция").

The geographic coverage is an elongated north–south corridor typical of a 1:200,000 sheet subdivision, showing a moderately dissected terrain with numerous geological contacts, fault systems, and mineralization zones.

---

## Key Content

**Geological Framework:**
- The map is dominated by Precambrian and Riphean-age metamorphic and igneous units, labeled with stratigraphic codes such as **R₁ar** (Riphean, Aral series?), **R₂am** (Riphean, Aman series?), **R₃am**, **R₃fo**, **C₁ar**, and **KZ** (Cenozoic cover). These units form elongated NNW–SSE trending belts, consistent with the structural grain of Central Kazakhstan's Precambrian basement.
- **Fault systems** are prominently shown: red dashed and solid lines indicate major fault zones trending NW–SE and NNW–SSE. Pink/red diagonal hatching in the northwestern portion of the map likely denotes a **fault zone or shear corridor** of regional significance.
- **Cenozoic cover** (KZ) is shown as blank or lightly marked areas, particularly in the central and eastern portions, obscuring the basement geology beneath sedimentary overburden.

**Mineralization and Prognosis Zonation:**
- The most prominent feature is a **hatched polygonal zone** in the west-central part of the map, rendered in **dark horizontal hatching (black lines on red/pink background)**. This represents a **prognosis (forecast) area** — likely a zone of predicted or confirmed mineral concentration. It is labeled with Roman numerals **IB** and **II**, suggesting different categories or ranks of mineral prospectivity (e.g., Category IB = confirmed/inferred resource zone; Category II = prospective zone).
- Within and adjacent to this hatched zone, several **circular symbols** (filled and open circles) are visible, representing **known mineral occurrences or deposits** — likely copper (Cu), given the annotation "●Cu" visible at several localities across the map.
- **Green elongated lensoid symbols** scattered across the map (particularly in the western half) likely represent **ore-bearing or anomalous zones**, possibly skarn bodies, hydrothermal veins, or geochemical anomalies.
- The notation **2Г** in the southwestern quadrant likely denotes a **prognosis zone of Category 2G**, a Soviet classification for inferred mineral resources with geological basis but without direct confirmation.

**Structural Patterns:**
- Folded Riphean metasedimentary belts (R₂am, R₃am sequences) trend NNW, intruded by granitoid bodies.
- The fault network creates a classic **transpressive corridor** setting favorable for hydrothermal Cu (and possibly Mo, Au) mineralization.
- Contour lines in the southern portion of the map (values around ~000 intervals) suggest **topographic or isoline data** (possibly structural contours or geophysical isolines) integrated into the geological base.

**Legend Reference:**
- The note at the bottom states: **"Условные обозначения см. на чертеже №33"** — meaning the full legend/key is on Drawing No. 33 (a separate sheet in the same report), so this map must be read in conjunction with that legend sheet.
